text:
Admission Date:  1972-05-30              Discharge Date:   1996-10-31

Date of Birth:  2005-01-13             Sex:   F

Service: CARDIOTHORACIC

Allergies:
Morphine

Attending:Heather
Chief Complaint:
tracheobronchial malacia

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
right thoracotomy for tracheoplasty
bronchoscopy


History of Present Illness:
Ms. White Gomez is a 35-year-
old woman who has had history of asthma and recurrent
pulmonary infections. These have been worsening over time.
She underwent evaluation by an outside pulmonologist who
placed a tracheal bronchial Y stent. After placement of this
her symptomatology improved markedly. The Y stent had been
placed for some mild tracheobronchomalacia as evidenced by
dynamic maneuvers. Unfortunately Ms. White Gomez suffered a
fungal infection of the stent and required removal and
several weeks of antifungal therapy. We performed a repeat
bronchoscopy with dynamic maneuvers here and she was found to
have some distal tracheal malacia as well as left greater
than right mainstem malacia. Because of her symptomatic
relief with the stent but the intolerance to the stent, in
terms of the complication of fungal infection, we thought the
long-term option of chronic stent placement was not a good
one and thus we discussed the risks and benefits of moving
towards a surgical tracheobronchoplasty in order to reform
the airway and prevent collapse with forced exhalation.


Past Medical History:
 TBM, SEVERE asthma, reflux
PSH: Nissen, Howell wit BAL 12-27, rigid bronch 12-27, y-stent
removal 10-21


Family History:
non-contributory

Physical Exam:
General: well appearing 35 YO F in NAD
HEENT: unrenarkable
Chest: CTA bilat. right thoracotomy site- clean, dry and intact.
Gates drain site benign.
COR: RRR S1, S2
abd: soft, round, NT, ND, +BS
extrem: no LE edema
neuro: intact

Pertinent Results:
CXR 1986-04-13
Compared to the prior study, there is a deeper level of
inspiration, persistent and unchanged right apical PTX and no
significant interval change. Left basilar atelectasis again
seen.


Brief Hospital Course:
Pt was admitted and taken to the OR for tracheobronchialplasty
on 10-07. An epidural wa splaced for pain control w/ good effect.
[**Initials (NamePattern4) **] Ortega drain was placed in the right chest for drainage and was
maintained on sxn. Immediately post op was admitted to the SICU
for bronchospasm post extubation. Was treated aggressively w/
nebs w/ improvement.
POD#3 Hill drain was placed to water seal. Pt was transferred
from the ICU.
POD#4 an attempt to cap and flag epidural was unsuccessful. Pt's
pain  was unable to be managed w/ po pain medication and
supplemental IV pain med. Epidural was resumed.
POD#5 Gates drain was d/c'd and CXR revealed small stable right
PTX.
POD#6 episural was removed and pain was adeq controlled on po
percocet and ultram. RA amb sat 94%.

Medications on Admission:
 albuterol, advair, singulair,

Discharge Medications:
1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2
times a day).
2. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day) as needed.
3. Advair Diskus 250-50 mcg/Dose Disk with Device Sig: One (1)
Inhalation twice a day.
4. Singulair 10 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO once a day.
5. Tramadol 50 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q6H (every 6
hours) as needed.
Disp:*90 Tablet(s)* Refills:*1*
6. Oxycodone-Acetaminophen 5-325 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO
Q4H (every 4 hours) as needed.
Disp:*75 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
7. Albuterol Sulfate 2.5 mg/3 mL Solution for Nebulization Sig:
2.5 mgs Inhalation Q4H (every 4 hours) as needed for and PRN.
Disp:*80 doses* Refills:*0*
8. Ipratropium Bromide 0.02 % Solution Sig: One (1) neb
Inhalation Q4H (every 4 hours) as needed for and PRN.
Disp:*80 neb* Refills:*0*
9. Pantoprazole 40 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig: One
(1) Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO Q24H (every 24 hours).
Disp:*30 Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.)(s)* Refills:*1*
10. Mucinex 1,200 mg Tab, Multiphasic Release 12 hr Sig: One (1)
Tab, Multiphasic Release 12 hr PO twice a day.
Disp:*30 Tab, Multiphasic Release 12 hr(s)* Refills:*1*


Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
TBM, SEVERE asthma, reflux
PSH: Nissen, Howell wit BAL 12-27, rigid bronch 12-27, y-stent
removal 10-21
s/p tracheoplasty


Discharge Condition:
good


Discharge Instructions:
Call Dr.Sandra Contreras office 632-80-677 if you develop chest
pain, shortness of breath, fever, chills, swelling in neck,
redness or drainage from chest incision.

Followup Instructions:
You have a follow up appointment with Dr. Wilson
632-80-677 on the ICU Cardiology clinical center on
2007-04-06 at 3pm. Do not eat or drink after midnight on wednesday
in preparation for a bronchoscopy on thursday. You will need to
bring someone to drive you home on thursday.
